explanation:
The contract change: `get_name_in_module` now returns a tuple `(obj, module_name)` instead of just `obj`. The coordinated caller `get_monkeytype_config` is updated to unpack the tuple. All other callers still expect just the object to be returned directly, so they will break — for example, `type_from_dict` will try to use the tuple as a type, `from_callable` will do isinstance checks on a tuple, and the tests will fail because they compare the result directly to the expected object or expect `NameLookupError` to be raised (but instead get a tuple that doesn't match).

Target function:
